question: How many tracks are in the 'Rock' genre?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) as TrackCount FROM Track t JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId WHERE g.Name = 'Rock'

question: What is the total revenue for each customer support representative?
gold_sql: SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName as EmployeeName, SUM(i.Total) as TotalRevenue FROM Employee e JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC
gold_answer: Jane Peacock ($1,731.51), Margaret Park ($1,584.00), Steve Johnson ($1,393.92)

question: Which album has the most tracks, and how many tracks does it have?
gold_sql: SELECT al.Title, COUNT(t.TrackId) as TrackCount FROM Album al JOIN Track t ON al.AlbumId = t.AlbumId GROUP BY al.AlbumId ORDER BY TrackCount DESC LIMIT 1

question: For each genre, what is the average track length in milliseconds?
gold_sql: SELECT g.Name, AVG(t.Milliseconds) as AvgLength FROM Genre g JOIN Track t ON g.GenreId = t.GenreId GROUP BY g.GenreId ORDER BY AvgLength DESC
gold_answer: Various genres with average lengths ranging from ~200,000ms to ~400,000ms

question: Which artists have no albums in the database?
gold_sql: SELECT ar.Name FROM Artist ar LEFT JOIN Album al ON ar.ArtistId = al.ArtistId WHERE al.AlbumId IS NULL
gold_answer: Several artists have no albums (e.g., Milton Nascimento, Antônio Carlos Jobim, Azymuth)

question: What is the total revenue per month in 2021, and which month had the highest revenue?
gold_sql: SELECT strftime('%m', InvoiceDate) as Month, SUM(Total) as MonthlyRevenue FROM Invoice WHERE strftime('%Y', InvoiceDate) = '2021' GROUP BY Month ORDER BY MonthlyRevenue DESC
gold_answer: Month 05 (May) had the highest revenue at $87.13

question: How does the average invoice total differ between customers from the USA and customers from Canada?
gold_sql: SELECT BillingCountry, AVG(Total) as AvgInvoice FROM Invoice WHERE BillingCountry IN ('USA', 'Canada') GROUP BY BillingCountry

question: Which playlist has the most unique tracks, and how many?
gold_sql: SELECT p.Name, COUNT(DISTINCT pt.TrackId) as UniqueTrackCount FROM Playlist p JOIN PlaylistTrack pt ON p.PlaylistId = pt.PlaylistId GROUP BY p.PlaylistId ORDER BY UniqueTrackCount DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which artists have tracks in the 'Jazz' genre but no tracks in the 'Rock' genre?
gold_sql: SELECT ar.Name FROM Artist ar WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Track t JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId JOIN Album al ON t.AlbumId = al.AlbumId WHERE al.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId AND g.Name = 'Jazz') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Track t2 JOIN Genre g2 ON t2.GenreId = g2.GenreId JOIN Album al2 ON t2.AlbumId = al2.AlbumId WHERE al2.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId AND g2.Name = 'Rock')
gold_answer: Antônio Carlos Jobim, Billy Cobham, Gilberto Gil, Spyro Gyra, Miles Davis, Gene Krupa, Dennis Chambers, Incognito, Aisha Duo, Aaron Goldberg

question: For each customer, list their top 3 genres by total spending
gold_sql: WITH CustomerGenreSpending AS (SELECT c.CustomerId, c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName as CustomerName, g.Name as Genre, SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity) as TotalSpent, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY c.CustomerId ORDER BY SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity) DESC) as rn FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId JOIN InvoiceLine il ON i.InvoiceId = il.InvoiceId JOIN Track t ON il.TrackId = t.TrackId JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId GROUP BY c.CustomerId, g.GenreId) SELECT CustomerName, Genre, TotalSpent FROM CustomerGenreSpending WHERE rn <= 3 ORDER BY CustomerName, TotalSpent DESC
gold_answer: Per-customer top 3 genres by spending (e.g., Aaron Mitchell: Latin $13.86, Rock $8.91, Metal $6.93)

question: Which playlists contain tracks from more than 5 different artists?
gold_sql: SELECT p.Name, COUNT(DISTINCT ar.ArtistId) as ArtistCount FROM Playlist p JOIN PlaylistTrack pt ON p.PlaylistId = pt.PlaylistId JOIN Track t ON pt.TrackId = t.TrackId JOIN Album al ON t.AlbumId = al.AlbumId JOIN Artist ar ON al.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId GROUP BY p.PlaylistId, p.Name HAVING ArtistCount > 5 ORDER BY ArtistCount DESC
gold_answer: Music (198), 90's Music (109), Classical (67), Classical 101 - Deep Cuts (25), Classical 101 - The Basics (25), Classical 101 - Next Steps (23), Brazilian Music (12), Heavy Metal Classic (9), TV Shows (6), TV Shows (6), Grunge (6)

question: Total revenue by artist, including artists with $0 revenue
gold_sql: SELECT ar.Name as ArtistName, COALESCE(SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity), 0) as TotalRevenue FROM Artist ar LEFT JOIN Album al ON ar.ArtistId = al.ArtistId LEFT JOIN Track t ON al.AlbumId = t.AlbumId LEFT JOIN InvoiceLine il ON t.TrackId = il.TrackId GROUP BY ar.ArtistId, ar.Name ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC
gold_answer: Iron Maiden ($138.60), U2 ($105.93), Metallica ($90.09), Led Zeppelin ($86.13), Lost ($81.59) ... plus many artists at $0

question: Which customers have purchased tracks from every genre that 'AC/DC' has tracks in?
gold_sql: WITH acdc_genres AS (SELECT DISTINCT g.GenreId FROM Genre g JOIN Track t ON g.GenreId = t.GenreId JOIN Album al ON t.AlbumId = al.AlbumId JOIN Artist ar ON al.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId WHERE ar.Name = 'AC/DC'), customer_genres AS (SELECT c.CustomerId, c.FirstName || ' ' || c.LastName as CustomerName, g.GenreId FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId JOIN InvoiceLine il ON i.InvoiceId = il.InvoiceId JOIN Track t ON il.TrackId = t.TrackId JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId GROUP BY c.CustomerId, g.GenreId) SELECT cg.CustomerName FROM customer_genres cg JOIN acdc_genres ag ON cg.GenreId = ag.GenreId GROUP BY cg.CustomerId, cg.CustomerName HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT cg.GenreId) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM acdc_genres)
gold_answer: Luís Gonçalves, Leonie Köhler, François Tremblay, Bjørn Hansen, František Wichterlová, Helena Holý, Astrid Gruber, Daan Peeters, Kara Nielsen, Eduardo Martins (and more)

question: What is the month-over-month revenue growth rate for 2021?
gold_sql: WITH monthly AS (SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', InvoiceDate) as Month, SUM(Total) as Revenue FROM Invoice WHERE strftime('%Y', InvoiceDate) = '2021' GROUP BY Month) SELECT Month, Revenue, CASE WHEN LAG(Revenue) OVER (ORDER BY Month) IS NULL THEN NULL WHEN LAG(Revenue) OVER (ORDER BY Month) = 0 THEN NULL ELSE ROUND(((Revenue - LAG(Revenue) OVER (ORDER BY Month)) * 100.0 / LAG(Revenue) OVER (ORDER BY Month)), 2) END as GrowthRate FROM monthly ORDER BY Month

question: Which support representative has the highest average invoice value for customers in their territory?
gold_sql: SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName as EmployeeName, AVG(i.Total) as AvgInvoiceValue FROM Employee e JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId, e.FirstName, e.LastName ORDER BY AvgInvoiceValue DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which tracks appear in more playlists than the average track?
gold_sql: WITH track_playlist_counts AS (SELECT t.TrackId, t.Name, COUNT(DISTINCT pt.PlaylistId) as PlaylistCount FROM Track t LEFT JOIN PlaylistTrack pt ON t.TrackId = pt.TrackId GROUP BY t.TrackId, t.Name), avg_playlists AS (SELECT AVG(PlaylistCount) as AvgCount FROM track_playlist_counts) SELECT tpc.Name, tpc.PlaylistCount FROM track_playlist_counts tpc, avg_playlists ap WHERE tpc.PlaylistCount > ap.AvgCount ORDER BY tpc.PlaylistCount DESC
gold_answer: Classical tracks appearing in 5 playlists each (e.g., 'Intoitus: Adorate Deum', 'Miserere mei, Deus')

expected_result.Name: Aria Mit 30 Veränderungen, BWV 988 "Goldberg Variations": Aria
